perturbation.original_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement (hereinafter referred to as: “Contract” or “Agreement”) was freely stipulated and entered into without any pressure or duress whatsoever as of (insert effective date), between: JB Machine LLC , having primary place of residence at 14309 Toepperwein rd ste 403 San Antonio, TX 78233 (hereinafter referred to as “Recipient ” or “Company ”), And (Recipient ), having primary place of residence at ___________________________________________________________________________ ___________________________________________________________________________ __________________________________________________________________ _________ (hereinafter referred to as “Owner ” or “Client ”), (Collectively referred to as: “Parties”, “Contractors”)
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement (hereinafter referred to as: “Contract” or “Agreement”) was freely stipulated and entered into without any pressure or duress whatsoever as of (insert effective date), between: JB Machine LLC , having primary place of residence at 14309 Toepperwein rd ste 403 San Antonio, TX 78233 (hereinafter referred to as “Recipient ” or “Subsidiary ”), And (Recipient ), having primary place of residence at ___________________________________________________________________________ ___________________________________________________________________________ __________________________________________________________________ _________ (hereinafter referred to as “Owner ” or “Client ”), (Collectively referred to as: “Parties”, “Contractors”)
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Company' is replaced with 'Subsidiary' as an alternative for 'Recipient'. This creates uncertainty because the rest of the document refers to the 'Recipient' and 'Company' interchangeably. It's unclear if 'Subsidiary' carries the same obligations. This is conflicting later, because there are references to company and what the reciepient should do in regards to the company, therefore 'Subsidiary' does not have the same legal implications to be considered interchangeable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company respects the legitima te interests of the Client, and is willing to abide by the terms set out in this contract.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Company' interchangeably with 'Recipient', while the changed text replaces 'Company' with 'Subsidiary' as an alternative for 'Recipient'. The contradicted text states 'The Company respects the legitimate interests of the Client...'. Because the term 'Company' may no longer automatically refer to the 'Recipient' due to the introduction of 'Subsidiary', it is unclear whether the 'Subsidiary' also respects the legitimate interests of the Client, creating a contradiction. Therefore, the term 'Subsidiary' does not have the same legal implications as 'Company' or 'Recipient'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as it misaligns terminology and creates conflicting obligations, as per the definition of the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: SOURCE OF INFORMATION The information received by the Recipien t is primarily from execution of a standalone contract connected to the Recipient’s business. The Recipient is, by all objective standards a legitimate and legally bound private entity established under applicable US law. Within the scope of the business activity of the Recipient, who works and operates in the manufacturing and wholesale business, the existent agreement between the parties, more specifically, its execution does require certain confidential information from the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: SOURCE OF INFORMATION The information received by the Recipien t is primarily from execution of a standalone contract connected to the Recipient’s projects. The Recipient is, by all objective standards a legitimate and legally bound private entity established under applicable US law. Within the scope of the business activity of the Recipient, who works and operates in the manufacturing and wholesale business, the existent agreement between the parties, more specifically, its execution does require certain confidential information from the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'business' to 'projects' introduces ambiguity. Later sections assume the information is related to the 'Recipient's business', but the term is now redefined to imply a project basis, narrowing the scope and potentially excluding information connected to general business operations. This is contradictory because it affects what information is deemed confidential. This is contradicting in the part where it determines which are considered confidential information
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Confidential information” ( abbreviated : “information”) means any information or material which is proprietary to the Owner, whether or not owned or developed by the Owner, w hich is generally not known to anyone but the Owner, and which the Recipient is to obtain by fulfilling his/hers duties. The term is also to be used for information , metric units, useful designs, technical data, expertise , descriptions, plans, schemes, sketches , or other information, which is proprietary to the Owner of the classified information. Confidential information will include any information provided, directly or indirectly, by the owner, as well as information regarding various parameters of per formance, procedure or other relevant characteristic, which is tied to the relevant position. Confidential information also includes any information of the owner and a third party with which the Owner deals, including, but not limited to: financial informa tion, business records, plans, trade secrets, product ideas, technical data of any type, contracts, billing records, pricing structure, discounts, property, investments , strategic alliances , partnerships, customer or client lists. The nature of the informa tion and manner of the disclosure are such that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential. Confidential information is also to include information, which is not to be clearly classified as such, and are connected to the execution of contract on behalf of the Recipient of confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'business' to 'projects' in the 'SOURCE OF INFORMATION' section creates a contradiction with the 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' section. The original text tied the confidential information to the Recipient's 'business,' implying a broad scope. The modified text narrows it to the Recipient's 'projects,' potentially excluding information related to general business operations. This directly contradicts the 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' section, which broadly defines confidential information as including financial information, business records, plans, trade secrets, and contracts related to the owner's business. The narrower scope introduced by 'projects' creates ambiguity and inconsistency, making it unclear whether information related to the Recipient's general business operations (but not directly tied to a specific project) is considered confidential. This is a strong contradiction because it affects what information is deemed confidential, impacting the obligations and rights of the parties.

perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Confidential information” ( abbreviated : “information”) means any information or material which is proprietary to the Owner, whether or not owned or developed by the Owner, w hich is generally not known to anyone but the Owner, and which the Recipient is to obtain by fulfilling his/hers duties. The term is also to be used for information , metric units, useful designs, technical data, expertise , descriptions, plans, schemes, sketches , or other information, which is proprietary to the Owner of the classified information. Confidential information will include any information provided, directly or indirectly, by the owner, as well as information regarding various parameters of per formance, procedure or other relevant characteristic, which is tied to the relevant position.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Confidential information” ( abbreviated : “records”) means any information or material which is proprietary to the Owner, whether or not owned or developed by the Owner, w hich is generally not known to anyone but the Owner, and which the Recipient is to obtain by fulfilling his/hers duties. The term is also to be used for information , metric units, useful designs, technical data, expertise , descriptions, plans, schemes, sketches , or other information, which is proprietary to the Owner of the classified information. Confidential information will include any information provided, directly or indirectly, by the owner, as well as information regarding various parameters of per formance, procedure or other relevant characteristic, which is tied to the relevant position.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the abbreviation from "information” to “records” creates internal inconsistency. The document continues to use “information” elsewhere when referring to confidential information, potentially leading to confusion and disputes over which term is meant in different contexts. This is contradicting in many locations on the text due to this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXCEPTIONS FROM CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Term “Confidential information” does NOT include: - Matter of public or otherwise freely accessible knowledge, resulting from direct disclosure by the Owne r - Information rightfully received by the Recipient from a Third party without any duty of confidentiality - Independently developed information on behalf of the Recipient - Already d isclosed information for the purposes of court or civil proceedings, respecting the principle of “minimal disclosure” - Information disclosed on behalf of the Recipient with prior written or oral ascent from the Owner
perturbation.justification: The change in the abbreviation from 'information' to 'records' creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'Confidential information'. Because the definition of "Confidential information" now uses 'records', subsequent references to "Confidential information" in other sections, like the contradicted text, become ambiguous. The contradicted section refers to exceptions to "Confidential information" and it references the term “Confidential information”. If that term is now abbreviated to “records” in the defining section, it causes confusion because the term information is still used elsewhere. This contradicts the definition of "Confidential information" because it no longer aligns with the intended term. Therefore, there is a direct conflict between the modified text and the contradicted text, which would lead to inconsistencies.

perturbation.original_text: OBLIGATION OF PROTECTION The Recipient hereby understand the sensitive nature of the information, and thus acknow ledges that the information is a valuable, special and unique asses proprietary to the Owner, which provides the Owner with a significant competitive advantage, and thus needs to be protected from improper disclosure. In consideration for the receipt b y the Recipient of information, the Recipient agrees to: - Hold the information in confidence and will not, in any way, disclose the information to any person or entity without prior written ascent of the Owner - Not copy or, in any way, modify any information without the prior written ascent of the Owner - The Recipient is to promptly advise the Owner if the Recipient becomes aware of an possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the information - The Recipient is not to disclose any information to any employees o f the Recipient, aside from the employees required - To have the information in order to perform their job duties in connection with the limited purposes of this Agreement. Each authorized Employee to whom confidential information is disclosed shall sign a non-disclosure agreement substantially same as this agreement upon request of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: OBLIGATION OF PROTECTION The Recipient hereby understand the sensitive nature of the materials, and thus acknow ledges that the information is a valuable, special and unique asses proprietary to the Owner, which provides the Owner with a significant competitive advantage, and thus needs to be protected from improper disclosure. In consideration for the receipt b y the Recipient of information, the Recipient agrees to: - Hold the information in confidence and will not, in any way, disclose the information to any person or entity without prior written ascent of the Owner - Not copy or, in any way, modify any information without the prior written ascent of the Owner - The Recipient is to promptly advise the Owner if the Recipient becomes aware of an possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the information - The Recipient is not to disclose any information to any employees o f the Recipient, aside from the employees required - To have the information in order to perform their job duties in connection with the limited purposes of this Agreement. Each authorized Employee to whom confidential information is disclosed shall sign a non-disclosure agreement substantially same as this agreement upon request of the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'materials' in the first sentence creates a contradiction, because the rest of the paragraph refers to protecting 'information'. It is unclear whether this obligation applies to all information or only specific 'materials', leading to uncertainty. This contradiction is on the following sentences after the first one.
perturbation.contradicted_text: OBLIGATION OF PROTECTION The Recipient hereby understand the sensitive nature of the materials, and thus acknow ledges that the information is a valuable, special and unique asses proprietary to the Owner, which provides the Owner with a significant competitive advantage, and thus needs to be protected from improper disclosure. In consideration for the receipt b y the Recipient of information, the Recipient agrees to: - Hold the information in confidence and will not, in any way, disclose the information to any person or entity without prior written ascent of the Owner - Not copy or, in any way, modify any information without the prior written ascent of the Owner - The Recipient is to promptly advise the Owner if the Recipient becomes aware of an possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the information - The Recipient is not to disclose any information to any employees o f the Recipient, aside from the employees required - To have the information in order to perform their job duties in connection with the limited purposes of this Agreement. Each authorized Employee to whom confidential information is disclosed shall sign a non-disclosure agreement substantially same as this agreement upon request of the Owner.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'materials' in the first sentence of the 'OBLIGATION OF PROTECTION' section creates a contradiction. The subsequent sentences, as well as the contradicted text itself, refer back to 'information'. This discrepancy introduces ambiguity as to whether the obligation of protection applies only to 'materials' or to all 'information' received by the Recipient, which directly conflicts with the subsequent sentences that refer to protecting 'information'. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the interchangeable use of terms leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: RIGHT TO INJUCTIVE RELIEF If it appears that the Recipient has, or has threatened to, disclose any confidential information in violation of this Agreement’s provisions, the owner shall be entitled to an injunctive relief restraining the Recipient form further or any such disclosure. The Owner is not limited to seeking any other forms of legal remedy in sole discretion and as seen fit by the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: RIGHT TO INJUCTIVE RELIEF If it appears that the Recipient has, or has threatened to, disclose any confidential data in violation of this Agreement’s provisions, the owner shall be entitled to an injunctive relief restraining the Recipient form further or any such disclosure. The Owner is not limited to seeking any other forms of legal remedy in sole discretion and as seen fit by the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "confidential information" with "confidential data" introduces another term for the same concept without clarifying the relationship between them. If data is a subset or superset of information, it can complicate enforcement. It is conflicting later in the next section.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Section: PROTECTION OF INTEREST . DISPOSAL OF INFORMATION
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROTECTION OF INTEREST . DISPOSAL OF INFORMATION The Recipient is to protect any type of information, whether fitting the definition of “confidential information” or no t, if he or she believes that disclosing this information may lead to a loss or damage of any kind to the Owner. The Recipient is to dispose of paperwork, data, discs, memory cards, e -mails or any copies of modifications made upon previous written ascent f rom the Owner in a manner prescribed by the Owner’s disposal of information policy. The Recipient shall be informed on the protocols of discarding or disposing of any tangible medium upon request.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'confidential information' to 'confidential data' introduces a new term without defining its relationship to the original term. The contradicted text refers to 'information, whether fitting the definition of “confidential information” or not' and 'data'. The introduction of 'confidential data' creates ambiguity about whether 'confidential data' is a subset, superset, or equivalent to 'confidential information'. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, specifically that multiple terms are used interchangeably without clear definitions, leading to conflicting obligations regarding the protection and disposal of these items. A reasonable reader may not know how to apply the data protections listed in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION On demand, at any time and at any event which may occur the Recipient is obl iged to immediately deliver to the Owner all the date, manuals, lists, notes, writings, product lists, photocopies, recordings, disks or other material (including duplicates and/or copies of any such property and/or material) concerning the work at hand. T he Recipient understands and respects that the Owner has all proprietary rights to these materials, and that these materials are entrusted to the Recipient on basis of execution of contract tied to this Non -disclosure agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION On demand, at any time and at any event which may occur the Recipient is obl iged to immediately deliver to the Owner all the date, manuals, lists, notes, writings, product lists, photocopies, recordings, disks or other documentation (including duplicates and/or copies of any such property and/or material) concerning the work at hand. T he Recipient understands and respects that the Owner has all proprietary rights to these materials, and that these materials are entrusted to the Recipient on basis of execution of contract tied to this Non -disclosure agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term 'material' to 'documentation' could exclude certain types of information that aren't considered 'documentation,' but were originally covered under 'material.' This makes the obligation to return confidential information ambiguous. The word 'documentation' restricts the original broader meaning of the term 'material', therefore is contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION On demand, at any time and at any event which may occur the Recipient is obl iged to immediately deliver to the Owner all the date, manuals, lists, notes, writings, product lists, photocopies, recordings, disks or other documentation (including duplicates and/or copies of any such property and/or material) concerning the work at hand. T he Recipient understands and respects that the Owner has all proprietary rights to these materials, and that these materials are entrusted to the Recipient on basis of execution of contract tied to this Non -disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the term 'material', which is broad. The changed text replaces 'material' with 'documentation', narrowing the scope of what must be returned. The contradicted text uses both 'documentation' and 'material'. The explanation specifies that documentation restricts the broader meaning of the term material. Therefore, the change causes an in-text contradiction by creating ambiguity and potentially excluding items previously covered under 'material', which is still used in the contradicted text.
